intro: Real, sourced from Kelly Benefits' internal process documentation — not an inferred model.

phases.title: Enrollment & Notice Setup
phases.summary: What happens before anyone ever has a qualifying event — the plan documentation and rights notices every enrolled employee is owed from day one.

steps.text: Group health plan provides a Summary Plan Description (SPD) to participant
steps.time: Within 90 days after person becomes participant

steps.text: Kelly Benefits mails General Notice of COBRA Rights to enrolled employee and spouse/dependents (if any)
steps.time: Generated within a week after enrollment (required within 90 days after person becomes participant)

steps.text: Enrolled employee or dependent requests any plan documents
Group health plan provides requested plan documents
steps.time: Within 30 days of request

phases.title: Qualifying Event → Election
phases.summary: From the client's notification of a qualifying event through the qualified beneficiary's decision to elect or decline.

steps.text: Client notifies Kelly Benefits of an employee and/or dependent's COBRA qualifying event
Kelly Benefits sends a COBRA election notice to qualified beneficiaries
steps.time: Generated within a week of termination being processed

steps.text: Qualified beneficiary elects COBRA coverage, or does not elect (to elect within at least 60 days of the election notice being sent, or the date of coverage loss, whichever is later)
Qualified beneficiary does not receive COBRA coverage. No further action.
steps.time: N/A

phases.title: Initial Premium & Enrollment
phases.summary: The two different paths a new COBRA enrollment takes depending on whether the initial premium arrives with the election or separately — and what happens if it's short or never comes.

steps.text: Initial premium payment due
steps.time: Within at least 45 days of COBRA election decision being sent

steps.text: Did the qualified beneficiary send the initial premium together with their election, or send the election alone?

Kelly Benefits (1) enrolls qualified beneficiary in COBRA coverage (in Admin and at carrier); (2) applies premium to their first coverage month; (3) generates first bill indicating payment made and mails it (including payment and KTBSonline instructions)
steps.time: (1) Typically 7–10 business days from receipt; (2) 1–3 business days; (3) when payment is applied

steps.text: → Continues into Active Coverage & Billing
Kelly Benefits enrolls qualified beneficiary in COBRA coverage (in Admin only, not at carrier); first bill is generated and mailed (including payment and KTBSonline instructions)
steps.time: Typically 7–10 business days from receipt

steps.text: Qualified beneficiary pays the initial premium, or does not
Kelly Benefits terminates coverage due to non-payment (no notice is sent, since the initial premium was never paid)
steps.time: Around the 5th of the following coverage month

steps.text: Kelly Benefits enrolls qualified beneficiary in COBRA coverage at the carrier
steps.time: Typically 7–10 business days from receipt of payment

steps.text: Is the payment short by the lesser of $50 or 10% of the required amount (an 'insignificant underpayment')?
Send a Notice of Insignificant Premium Underpayment email to the qualified beneficiary
steps.time: Around the 5th of the following coverage month

steps.text: Does the qualified beneficiary pay the remaining amount due within 2 weeks of the notice email?
Pays remaining amount due
steps.time: Within 2 weeks from notice email being sent

steps.text: Kelly Benefits terminates coverage due to non-payment
steps.time: Around the 5th of the following coverage month

steps.text: → Continues into Active Coverage & Billing

phases.title: Active Coverage & Ongoing Billing
phases.summary: The recurring monthly cycle once coverage is live — and the two ways it ends.

steps.text: Kelly Benefits generates monthly COBRA bill on KTBSonline, with payment due date. Tied to the generation of the client bill (i.e. if the client bill is restricted, the COBRA bill will be restricted)
steps.time: Typically by the 7th of the month prior to the coverage month

steps.text: Subsequent monthly premium payment due
steps.time: Due by the first day of the coverage month, or by the due date on the bill, with a 30-day grace period

steps.text: Qualified beneficiary pays premium, or does not
Coverage continues — cycle repeats monthly
Kelly Benefits terminates coverage due to non-payment and sends notice of early termination due to non-payment
steps.time: Around the 5th of the following coverage month (i.e. March non-pays are termed on 4/5); or 2 weeks from notice email being sent, if it was an insignificant underpayment case

steps.text: How does coverage end?
No further action
steps.time: N/A

steps.text: Kelly Benefits terminates qualified beneficiary's COBRA account
steps.time: Typically 7–10 business days from receipt of request

phases.title: Annual Renewal
phases.summary: What happens when the client's plans come up for renewal — a genuinely different process depending on which pod the client sits in, and whether the renewal was actively elected or defaulted.

steps.text: Plans are up for annual renewal — which pod is the client in?
Kelly Benefits mails a letter to the client providing instructions on what to provide to qualified beneficiaries for renewal, plus a census of active and pending beneficiaries
steps.time: 2 months prior to renewal

steps.text: The COBRA team emails the assigned account manager (KBS pod) / broker manager (LG pod) asking them to confirm by a specific date that they'll compile the COBRA Renewal Packet
steps.time: 2 months prior to renewal; confirmation requested by the 15th of that month

steps.text: Does the Account Manager / Broker Manager confirm they'll provide renewal info?
Compiles the COBRA Renewal Packet (new rates, benefit summaries, and renewal election form)
steps.responsibility: Account manager (KBS pod) / Broker manager (LG pod)
steps.time: By the 15th of the month prior to the renewal effective date

steps.text: Mails the Renewal Packet to qualified beneficiaries (or outsourced to a third-party printer for a Major Account client)
steps.time: Within 5 business days of receipt

steps.text: Kelly Benefits mails the generic Open Enrollment/Renewal Notification letter to the qualified beneficiary, asking them to reach out to the client for more info about the renewal
steps.time: 15th of the month prior to the renewal effective date

steps.text: Separately: Client compiles the new plan rates, benefit summaries, and renewal election form, and sends it to qualified beneficiaries
steps.time: Within 210 days after the end of the plan year in which changes became effective, or within 60 days after a material reduction is adopted (legal requirement — Kelly Benefits doesn't set the client's deadline)

steps.text: Kelly Benefits restricts the client's renewal month bill until renewal plans are installed / Open Enrollment is completed (this keeps COBRA direct-bills from generating during the transition)
steps.time: Typically by the 1st of the month prior to the renewal month

steps.text: Renewal plans installed / OE completed → Kelly Benefits reviews a preview bill for corrections (LG/KBS pods only) and unrestricts the client's renewal month bill
steps.responsibility: (1) Broker/account manager; (2) Dedicated service (LG/KBS) or Implementation (SG)
steps.time: When preview bill audit completed (LG/KBS), or when renewal plans are all installed (SG)

steps.text: Qualified beneficiary has renewal information and can elect plan/coverage-level changes
steps.time: Until the end of the renewal month

steps.text: Was there a plan or carrier change requiring an active election, or did everything renew as-is (enrollment defaulted)?
Does the qualified beneficiary take action?

If the qualified beneficiary has no active plans left (all plans changed at renewal and no election made), Kelly Benefits terminates the COBRA account and sends notice of early termination due to no renewal application. If they still have some active plans (partial default), no action is taken.
steps.time: End of the renewal month

steps.text: Kelly Benefits enrolls qualified beneficiaries in their elected plans
steps.time: Typically 7–10 business days from receipt of elections

steps.text: COBRA coverage continues for plans elected and/or defaulted
Does the qualified beneficiary have active coverage in the renewal month?

COBRA bill for the renewal month is generated and posted to KTBSonline, with (1) the renewal date as the due date if generated before the renewal date, or (2) 14 days after the bill generation date as the due date if generated after the renewal date
steps.time: When the client bill is generated

steps.text: No bill is generated

phases.title: Termination Date & Carrier Discrepancy Handling
phases.summary: What happens when a qualifying event's timing runs up against carrier retroactivity rules — a real source of friction between what's requested and what the carrier will actually approve.

steps.text: Does the notification of the qualifying event fall within the carrier's retroactivity rules, or exceed them?
Kelly Benefits terminates for the requested effective date
steps.time: (1) Within 5–7 business days of receipt from client, or (2) same day

steps.text: Kelly Benefits terminates for a current effective date, and requests the original effective date from the carrier (unless the carrier is CareFirst Facets)
steps.time: Within 5–7 business days of receipt from client

steps.text: How does the carrier respond?
Kelly Benefits updates to match the termination date approved by the carrier
steps.time: Within 5–7 business days of receipt from carrier

steps.text: Kelly Benefits updates to match the termination date approved by the carrier (original or alternate)
steps.time: Within 5–7 business days of receipt from carrier

branches.label: Rejects any date beyond a current effective date

steps.text: Kelly Benefits notifies the client that the member had to be terminated for a date other than the originally requested date
steps.responsibility: Dedicated Service (large groups) or MIF team (small groups)
steps.time: Within 5–7 business days of receipt from carrier
